channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to More Peace Talks
item.description: Fighting continued in southern Lebanon between Israel and Iranian-backed Hezbollah militants, even as Israeli and Lebanese diplomats met in Washington. The war in Lebanon has threatened to undermine the U.S.-Iran cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: How Peter Magyar Defeated Viktor Orban, a Former Ally, In Hungary’s Election
item.description: For years, Peter Magyar was a loyal ally of Viktor Orban, the far-right Hungarian leader. Then he changed sides — and defeated his former boss in a landslide victory on Sunday. Does he represent real change?
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peter Magyar campaigning in 2024 in Viktor Orban’s hometown, Felcsut.

item.title: As War Chills Economy, Dubai’s Most Vulnerable Bear the Cost
item.description: Migrant workers in the United Arab Emirates, whose hospitality industry has been hit hard by the war with Iran, are facing furloughs, pay cuts and even repatriation.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Natalie Naccache for The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents of Dubai neighborhoods like Satwa have been hit especially hard by the economic downturn.

item.title: What Happened After Denmark Adopted a Ruined City in Ukraine
item.description: The Danish government has contributed more than $250 million to Mykolaiv, as the Trump administration pushes a more business-focused plan.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer and Brendan Hoffman
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times

item.title: Mark Carney Seals a Majority Government and Remakes Canada’s Liberal Party
item.description: The rising star in global centrist politics has secured a majority in the Canadian Parliament. Critics are crying foul.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada speaking on Saturday at a Liberal Party convention in Montreal.

item.title: With Algeria Visit, Pope Leo Returns to His Augustinian Roots
item.description: Some theologians say the pontiff’s understanding of Augustine’s teachings helped inform his response to President Trump’s negative comments about him.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV presiding over Holy Mass at the Basilica of Saint Augustine in Annaba, Algeria, on Tuesday.

item.title: As Bahrain Cracked Down Amid War, a Death in Custody Sparked Outrage
item.description: The body of a man accused of spying for Iran, Mohammed al-Mousawi, showed signs of torture, witnesses said. The U.N. has called for an investigation.
item.dc:creator: Nazeeha Saeed and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rising over Manama, Bahrain’s capital, last month after what was reported to be a drone strike on an airport’s fuel storage facility.

item.title: Benin’s Romuald Wadagni Poised to Win Presidential Election
item.description: Romuald Wadagni received 94 percent of the vote, according to provisional results. He faces the challenge of a growing jihadist insurgency in the region.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
Economic Community of West African States (Ecowas)
item.media:description: Romuald Wadagni campaigning in Cotonou, Benin, last month. He has become the country’s new president, according to provisional results.

item.title: Russian Oil Revenues Nearly Doubled in March
item.description: The surge in revenue provided a critical lifeline for Moscow, which has struggled to fund the war in Ukraine amid record-high deficits.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A Panamanian-flagged ship carrying crude oil from Russia, moored off an oil refinery in Limay, Bataan province, the Philippines, this month.

item.title: Will Viktor Orban’s Legacy Live On in Brussels, Even Without Him?
item.description: Viktor Orban, Hungary’s newly ousted prime minister, helped to finance a Brussels think tank that pushes his populist vision. It will outlast him, at least for a while.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Koba Ryckewaert
item.media:description: Viktor Orban at an European Union summit meeting in Brussels, in March.

item.title: Europe Is Desperate for More Energy. Can Norway Come to the Rescue?
item.description: The war in Iran has once again exposed Europe’s energy vulnerability. A friendly source at home would seem to be ideal. But it’s not that easy.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Carina Johansen/NTB Scanpix, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The Johan Sverdrup oil field in the North Sea west of Stavanger, off the southwestern coast of Norway.

item.title: Ex-Student Wounds 16 in High School Shooting in Turkey
item.description: Officials said the teenage attacker killed himself when cornered by the police. The authorities are investigating possible motives.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Safak Timur
item.media:description: Security forces outside a high school in Siverek, southeastern Turkey, on Tuesday, after a shooting on the premises.

item.title: Northern Mariana Islands, Guam On Alert for ‘Extremely Dangerous’ Super Typhoon Sinlaku
item.description: The storm, named Sinlaku, was expected to make landfall on Tuesday evening in Tinian and Saipan, where tens of thousands of people were urged to shelter in place.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: In Myanmar, Even Flowers Stoke Fear
item.description: Myanmar’s generals have brutalized the country for decades. But they have come to dread garlands as a sign of resistance.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Daniel Berehulak
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:description: The bombs come at any time. The only constant is that they will.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman, Peter Eavis, Michael Crowley and Euan Ward

item.title: How a Times Reporter Has Covered a Turbulent Period in Latin America
item.description: Frances Robles, an international correspondent for The New York Times, has reported on the region for more than 25 years.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:description: The reporter Frances Robles, right, with the Rev. Thomas Ngigi at St. Theresa’s parish, a waterfront church in Black River, Jamaica, that was badly damaged by Hurricane Melissa last year.

item.title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku Slams Saipan, Causing Widespread Damage
item.description: But there were no early reports of casualties from the storm, which was named Sinlaku and was moving northwest of the region.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung
item.media:description: Debris scattered on Wednesday in Saipan, Northern Mariana Islands, as the super typhoon Sinlaku moved through.

item.title: How China’s Weapons Transfers to Iran Have Evolved Over Decades
item.description: China has denied claims this month by U.S. intelligence that it might have shipped arms to Iran. In recent years, China has supported Iran with dual-use parts.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Military delegates at an annual legislative session in Beijing last month.

item.title: Pope Leo’s Challenge in Cameroon: Show African Catholics How Much They Matter
item.description: Catholicism is growing fast on the continent, yet Africans play a comparatively small role in church leadership. Cameroon, which Leo XIV will visit Wednesday, shows the divide.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.media:description: Posing after Mass on Sunday at St. Joseph Metropolitan Cathedral in Bamenda, Cameroon. For some Catholics in the country, a visit by Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday will be a reminder of disparities in the church.

item.title: Ship ‘Spoofing’ in Strait of Hormuz May Compound Confusion
item.description: A new pattern of deceptive activity by some vessels around the critical waterway suggests the new American blockade is changing how some ships linked to Iran are behaving.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A cargo ship off the coast of Ras al-Khaimah, United Arab Emirates, on Monday.

item.title: Rubio Hosts Israel and Lebanon for Rare Meeting Shadowed by U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The gathering ended with encouraging words, even as Israel continued to refuse to halt its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meeting with officials from Lebanon and Israel at the State Department in Washington on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump’s Blockade Risks Upending an Emerging Détente With China
item.description: In a thinly veiled critique of the war in Iran, China’s leader said the world could not risk reverting “to the law of the jungle.”
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump meeting with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, in South Korea last October. They will meet again next month.

item.title: Carney Suspends Gas Tax in Canada as War Drives Up Fuel Costs
item.description: The prime minister said that his government was also investing in ways to boost oil and gas production.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada at a news conference in Ottawa, on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Says 2nd Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Could Take Place This Week
item.description: The president did not say who would represent the United States in a potential next round of direct talks, though he ruled out being personally involved.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Mario Ríos Montt, Bishop and Brother of Genocidal General, Dies at 94
item.description: In Guatemala, he led the Catholic Church’s human rights office. His brother, a dictator who took power in a coup, was convicted of crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Bishop Mario Ríos Montt in 1999 at a news conference on human-rights atrocities committed by the military in Guatemala. “What interests me is the truth, and come what may, the truth is the truth,” he said.

item.title: Rare Direct Talks Between Israel and Lebanon Focus on Hezbollah
item.description: A meeting between Israeli and Lebanese officials in Washington came as the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports entered its second day.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Euan Ward, Anushka Patil and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, center, speaking during a meeting with Lebanese and Israeli ambassadors at the State Department on Tuesday.

item.title: Hungary’s Populist Paradox
item.description: We look at what Viktor Orban’s defeat tells us about a model of government in which politics are more important than the economy.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla Will Visit D.C., New York and Virginia
item.description: The planned four-day state visit comes at a fraught time in the U.S.-U.K. relationship, following President Trump’s frequent belittling of the British prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Queen Camilla and King Charles III of Britain earlier this month.

item.title: Israel’s Spy Chief Addresses Criticism About the War With Iran
item.description: The Mossad head said Israel’s mission would not be complete until the Iranian regime was replaced, after criticism that the war has left Iran’s theocratic government in place.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Gabby Sobelman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Italy Suspends Defense Pact With Israel, Further Straining Ties
item.description: The move by Italy, once considered a strong ally of Israel, reflected growing anger over Israeli aggression in the Middle East. President Trump criticized Italy’s stance on the war.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in Rome last week.

item.title: China Offers Veiled Criticism of U.S. as Iran War Threatens Oil Imports
item.description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, said that the world cannot risk reverting “to the law of the jungle.” Beijing has taken a more active role diplomatically as the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz persists.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: China’s leader Xi Jinping has tried to maintain a neutral stance on the war in Iran as it balances its partnership with Tehran and managing tensions with the United States.

item.title: Middle East War Will Slow Global Economic Growth, I.M.F. Warns
item.description: The conflict could also fuel another bout of inflation, according to the International Monetary Fund.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: New International Monetary Fund projections showed a global economy that was stopped in its tracks by the U.S. war in Iran.

item.title: State of U.S. Blockade Is Unclear as Some Ships Transit Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Ship-tracking data showed that several vessels, including some that had been docked at Iranian ports, had moved through the strait as the U.S. military began its blockade.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Francesca Regalado
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A ship is seen in the Persian Gulf off the coast of Sharjah, United Arab Emirates, on Monday, the day after U.S.-Iran peace talks broke down.

item.title: In Leo, Trump Faces a Different Kind of Papal Opponent
item.description: Unlike his predecessor, Pope Leo XIV enjoys growing support from a broad swath of conservative Catholics.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Dias and Motoko Rich
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: At the Vatican, Pope Leo presents a new foil for President Trump.

item.title: Trump’s Latest Oil Blockade Brings Bigger Economic Risks
item.description: Oil markets shrugged it off, but the effort to hurt Iran could provoke retaliation that inflicts more damage on energy assets and the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A strike on the Bapco oil refinery in Bahrain in early March. A U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz may give Iran a reason to restart attacks on energy assets throughout the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Seek Way Forward in Rare Direct Talks
item.description: The talks, set for Tuesday in Washington, were not expected to produce an immediate breakthrough in the war between Hezbollah and Israel.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A building on Saturday in the Lebanese capital, Beirut, that was destroyed by Israeli strikes.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Euan Ward and Peter Eavis

item.title: Amid War, G.O.P. Puts Off Testimony From Senior Pentagon Commanders
item.description: House lawmakers had been scheduled to hear testimony next week from the head of military operations in the Middle East, but the Republican majority postponed the hearing until late May.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We are six weeks into this conflict, and we still haven’t gotten a public briefing from anyone in the administration about the war,” said Representative Adam Smith of Washington, the ranking Democrat on the Armed Services Committee.

item.title: South Korea Can’t Build Enough Stadiums to Host Its K-Pop Stars
item.description: South Korea doesn’t have enough arenas to show off its own A-list talent. That’s why BTS began its world tour in a once-neglected, 22-year-old soccer stadium.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:description: Fans gathering outside Goyang Stadium ahead of BTS’s world tour opener in Goyang, South Korea, last week.

item.title: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
item.description: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A ship in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. Begins a Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: European countries declined to take part in the action, which is designed to pressure Iran into making concessions by cutting off its oil income.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Peter Eavis, Jenny Gross and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A cargo ship off the coast of Ras al-Khaimah, United Arab Emirates, on Monday.

item.title: The Venezuela Model
item.description: President Trump calls the removal of Nicolás Maduro “the perfect scenario.” But not everything is going right for the U.S. in Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

item.title: As Internet Blackout Hits Day 45, Iranians Bemoan State Hypocrisy and Economic Risks
item.description: Iranians enduring the latest communications shutdown warn that it’s crippling businesses. Critics say officials flagrantly use the internet they block citizens from.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An internet blackout in Iran has shut down communications for most of its more than 90 million citizens.

item.title: Killer’s Parents Could Have Prevented Southport Attack in U.K., Report Says
item.description: An official inquiry said the perpetrator’s parents, as well as government agencies, had missed several chances to avert the deadly attack.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial for the victims of the attack, in which three young girls were murdered and 10 others were injured, in Southport, England, in 2024.

item.title: ‘We Finally Have Democracy’: Hungarians Erupt in Joy and Relief
item.description: Many Hungarians, exhausted by a struggling economy and worried about a move away from the European Union, were jubilant after Prime Minister Viktor Orban conceded. Some had wondered if he would.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Lili Rutai
item.media:description: Budapest was lit by torchlight as people gathered to celebrate the Hungarian election results on Sunday.

item.title: Two Iran-Linked Ships Passed Through Strait of Hormuz Ahead of U.S. Blockade
item.description: The vessels exited the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, hours before a U.S. naval blockade took effect.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz last week. Traffic in the waterway has slowed since the start of the conflict.

item.title: Many Popes Before Leo Have Been Entangled in Politics. Here Are 5 of Them.
item.description: Leo XIV, under fire from President Trump, has joined predecessors like Francis and John Paul II in confronting political issues.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Algiers on Monday with the Algerian president, Abdelmadjid Tebboune.

item.title: How Orban Lost His Touch, and Hungary’s Election
item.description: The election defeat for Prime Minister Viktor Orban is less the result of an ideological shift in Hungary, and more the playing out of a fundamental rule of politics.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: A campaign poster in Keszthely, Hungary, for Prime Minister Viktor Orban with the word “Mafia” spray painted on it.

item.title: Iran Blockade Sets Up a Test of Which Side Can Endure More Pain
item.description: President Trump is trying to choke off the country’s lifeline with a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But the Iranians are betting that his tolerance for political pain is limited.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Fuel prices reflected on a semi-truck in Aurora, Ore., last week. “Soon you’ll be nostalgic for $4 to $5 gas,” Iran’s top negotiator warned American consumers after peace talks fell apart over the weekend.

item.title: Hungary May No Longer Be Putin’s Ally, but It Can’t Afford a Full Break
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s defeat will make it harder for the Kremlin to divide Europe, but Moscow retains leverage through energy sales.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne
item.media:description: Images of Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia during a rally in Budapest last week.

item.title: UK Will Not Join U.S. Blockade of Strait of Hormuz, Starmer Says
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the U.K.’s diplomatic efforts are directed at keeping the vital waterway “open, not shut.”
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain, who visited British troops in the Middle East last week, said Monday he is focused on “bringing countries together to keep the straits open, not shut.”

item.title: To Open the Strait of Hormuz, Trump Wants to Blockade Iran. Experts Are Skeptical.
item.description: President Trump’s announcement of a U.S. blockade was his latest effort to pressure Iran to negotiate after direct talks over the weekend did not yield an agreement.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new billboard in Tehran on Monday with text that translates to “Forever in Iran’s hands” and “Trump couldn’t do a damn thing. Iran will have the control over the Strait of Hormuz forever.”

item.title: Israelis Don’t Feel Much Like Victors in War With Iran
item.description: The regime in Iran has not changed and the nuclear and missile threats have not been eliminated, leaving many Israelis to wonder what this was all for.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Activists held posters of war victims from around the region during a protest in Tel Aviv on Saturday.

item.title: Defeat of Orban Removes Ukraine’s Biggest Nemesis in Europe
item.description: The Hungarian leader’s pro-Moscow tilt cemented his reputation in Kyiv as a spoiler. Now, Ukraine hopes a 90-billion-euro loan he blocked will be disbursed.
item.dc:creator: Constant Méheut
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary addressed supporters in Budapest on Sunday. He has conceded defeat in a general election.

item.title: 4 Takeaways From Orban’s Defeat in Hungary’s Election
item.description: Economics and unhappiness with endemic corruption helped Peter Magyar, the challenger, secure victory. A visit by the U.S. vice president in support of Mr. Orban didn’t change things.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary, center, at an election event for his political party, Fidesz, on Sunday.

item.title: To Fill Drone Force Ranks, Russia Targets a New Demographic: Students
item.description: Recruitment sessions have taken place at hundreds of universities and colleges across the country.
item.dc:creator: Milana Mazaeva, Nataliya Vasilyeva and Paul Sonne
item.media:description: A billboard promoting contract military service in the Russian army’s unmanned systems forces, in St. Petersburg, Russia, in January. Recruiters have been recruiting Russian university students as drone operators using similarly lucrative offers.

item.title: Canada Special Elections 2026 Results: Carney’s Liberal Party Gains Majority
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney, constrained by leading a minority government for the past year, gained a majority in the House of Commons after special elections on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada at the Liberal Party’s convention in Montreal on Saturday. His agenda is focused on reducing Canada’s dependence on the United States.
